seoTitle: Dean Park vs Debenham — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dean Park and Debenham. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dean Park vs Debenham: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dean Park and Debenham.
heading: Dean Park vs Debenham — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dean Park, New South Wales and Debenham, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Debenham has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Dean Park (49/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dean Park or Debenham safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dean Park has a flood risk score of 30/100, while Debenham scores 20/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dean Park scores 64/100 for bushfire risk. Debenham scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dean Park or Debenham?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Debenham has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.78% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
